description: Forbidden namespaces/symbols for VaM plugin C# source. Update this list whenever VaM rejects a symbol or API at compile/load time.

rules.reason: VaM compile sandbox rejects System.IO namespace references (NamespaceRestriction).
VaM compile sandbox rejects System.Reflection namespace references (NamespaceRestriction).
VaM compile sandbox rejects UnityEngine.PlayerPrefs references (TypeRestriction).
Offline-only licensing/distribution policy: block direct network APIs in runtime plugins.
VaM compile sandbox rejects System.Environment references (TypeRestriction).
Offline-only licensing/distribution policy: block Unity web request APIs in runtime plugins.
Reflection pathway. Keep blocked unless explicitly proven safe in VaM.
Runtime type inspection pathway. Keep blocked unless explicitly proven safe in VaM.
Reflection-based instance creation. Keep blocked unless explicitly proven safe in VaM.
Assembly reflection API surface. Keep blocked unless explicitly proven safe in VaM.

runtimeProfiles.description: Runtime/input behavior differences tracked by guard config so desktop/VR quirks stay explicit and extendable.

profiles.label: Desktop (No HMD)
knownDifferences: JoystickControl stick axes may be unavailable or noisy without active VR controllers.
SuperController menu/grab navigation can be keyboard-driven in desktop mode.
VR-only palette/spawn interactions should not be treated as active desktop input paths.
guidance: Use explicit desktop key bindings for deterministic control testing.
Avoid relying on legacy Input.GetAxis fallback in normal runtime paths.

profiles.label: VR (Oculus)
knownDifferences: Primary interaction uses JoystickControl axes plus grab/menu button state.
Two-controller arbitration is required so an idle hand does not suppress the active hand.
Session spawn mode uses control-hand stick for depth/scale and select/trigger for placement.
guidance: Treat joystick and grab navigation modes as independent, restorable state.
Keep trigger/panel-focus gates explicit when reading scrub input.
